title: Just for You - Immigrants - Canada.ca
content:
## Just for You - Immigrants
Immigrants are eligible for health care coverage under the Canada Health Act, although there are waiting periods of up to 90 days in some provinces.
Our Health Care page has basic information on Canada's health care system including federal responsibilities and links to provincial and territorial health ministries.
For a list of resources about your health and the surrounding environment, food safety and consumer product safety, consult the Consumer Information page.
- Seeking Employment in Canada
- Public Health Agency of Canada -- Immunization
- Information for Travellers
- Physical Activity Guide
- Our section on mental health .
- The Metropolis Project is an international cooperative research project that studies the effects of immigration upon the immigrant and upon the society that the immigrant enters.
Women experience the Canadian health care system in unique ways because of their gender, race, socio-economic status and geographical location.
- "Abuse is wrong in any language" : multilingual poster and booklet for immigrant women who are suffering from abuse in a relationship or in a family.
- Women (Just For You)
Family Violence and Immigrant Women
- Isolated, Frightened & Forgotten: The Service Delivery Needs and Realitiesd of Immigrants and Refugee Women Who Are Battered
- Like A Wingless Bird: A Tribute to the Survival and Courage of Women Who Are Abused and Who Speak Neither English Nor French
- Government of Canada -- Information for Non-Canadians
- Citizenship and Immigration Canada (CIC)
- Call Centres -- Information on Canadian immigration or citizenship is available 24 hours a day, seven days a week.
Our goal is to make this page as useful as possible and your comments can help.
